definition: Equity returns in demographically declining economies are muted but not catastrophic, with demographic drag estimated at 1–2% annualized. The Nikkei experience is the outlier (confounded by bubble burst), not the base case, but starting valuations materially affect outcomes.
evidence: Germany's DAX produced ~6% annualized nominal returns during demographic deceleration at ~12x starting P/E
Demographic contribution to equity underperformance estimated at 1–2% annualized drag
Nikkei conflated demographics with extreme bubble burst
S&P 500 starts at ~20–21x, materially higher than Germany's entry point
open_questions: How the same demographic drag applied to a richly valued US market affects outcomes
Whether buybacks and margin expansion offset demographic revenue drag
